text: Hair salon and barbershop businesses are consistently profitable in Uganda. Women's salon startup: UGX 1,500,000-3,000,000 (hair dryer: UGX 150,000-300,000, flat iron: UGX 80,000-150,000, clippers: UGX 100,000-200,000, mirror + chairs: UGX 200,000-400,000, initial product stock: UGX 300,000-500,000, rent deposit: UGX 300,000-600,000). Men's barbershop can start lower: UGX 500,000-1,000,000 (clippers, mirror, chair, small rent). Revenue: women's braiding UGX 15,000-50,000, relaxer UGX 20,000-40,000, weave installation UGX 30,000-100,000+. Men's haircut UGX 5,000-15,000. Busy salon: UGX 800,000-2,500,000/month.

Salon marketing tips for Uganda: 1) Before/after photos on WhatsApp Status and Facebook — this is your best free marketing tool. 2) Offer 'bring a friend' discounts: 20% off if a customer brings a new client. 3) Weekends are peak — staff up for Saturday. 4) Sell hair products (oils, relaxers, accessories) for additional revenue — markup is 30-50%. 5) Partner with nearby clothing boutiques for cross-referrals. 6) Keep your salon clean and play good music — atmosphere matters as much as skill. 7) Train in trending styles by watching YouTube tutorials — customers always want what's new.
